# Scraped Source Text

- URL: https://www.jobs.tu-berlin.de/en/job-postings/201397
- Retrieved UTC: 2026-03-05T04:50:18.648121+00:00

## Main Content
Job Posting V-67/26: Research assistant - 0.75 working time – Job Postings at Technische Universität Berlin

Faculty V - Mechanical Engineering and Transport Systems, Institute of Psychology and Ergonomics / Cognitive Psychology and Ergonomics

Research assistant - 0.75 working time

## About us
The Brains, Bots, and Minds Lab at Technische Universität Berlin, directed by Prof. Dr. Eva Wiese, is seeking a highly motivated researcher to join the team beginning in Spring 2026. Our research focuses on the cognitive neuroscience of human–robot interaction, combining EEG/ERP methods, cognitive modeling, and behavioral paradigms targeting visual attention and working memory. The successful candidate will be supervised by Prof. Dr. Eva Wiese and Dr. Tobias Feldmann-Wüstefeld within the DFG-funded project "Neural correlates of trust in human-AI interaction." The project offers the opportunity to pursue a doctorate.

## Your responsibility
- Designing, conducting, and analyzing behavioral and neurophysiological experiments on topics, such as human–robot interaction, human–AI interaction, cognitive offloading, and social artificial intelligence
- Developing and implementing experimental paradigms using EEG/ERP, eye-tracking, and computational or cognitive modeling
- Collaborating with researchers across different disciplines including cognitive neuroscience, AI, robotics, and human–computer interaction
- Preparing, writing, and publishing scientific articles in peer-reviewed journals
- Presenting research findings at international conferences
- Supporting general lab activities, data management, and open-science practices

## Your profile
- Successfully completed academic university degree (Diplom, Master or equivalent) in psychology, cognitive or social neurosciences, computational neurosciences or related areas
- Experience with programming experiments (PsychoPy or Matlab PsychtoolBox)
- Statistical analysis of behavioral and neuroscientific data in R (or equivalent)
- Experience with collecting and analyzing EEG data
- Proven ability to do independent and responsible scientific work in accordance with ethical guidelines
- Experience with collecting and analyzing eye tracking data
- Excellent scientific writing and presentation skills
- Very good knowledge of English
- Willingness to work in an international and interdisciplinary team is a plus
- Experience with obtaining ethics approval is a plus
- Strong motivation to conduct empirical research desirable
- Excellent teamworking ability desirable
- Readiness to implement open science desirable
- Strong interest in research on human-machine interaction desirable

## How to apply
Please send your application with the reference number V-67/26 and the usual documents only by email (combined in a single pdf file) to Prof. Dr. Eva Wiese (sekretariat@kke.tu-berlin.de).

## Facts
- Published: 19.02.2026
- Category: Research assistant
- Start date: Earliest possible (Spring 2026)
- Duration: three years
- Full/Part-time: 0.75 working time
- Salary: Salary grade 13 TV-L Berliner Hochschulen
- Application deadline: 06.03.2026
- Reference number: V-67/26
- Contact: Prof. Dr. Eva Wiese, sekretariat@kke.tu-berlin.de
